message:
fix(scheduler): extend stale-entry skipping to all remaining pop methods

Completes the stale-entry filtering pattern across every scheduler pop
method that was missed in the initial fix (ac4d2e96). The previous commit
only covered per-lane pop methods (pop_cancel_with_rng,
pop_timed_only_with_hint, pop_ready_only, pop_ready_only_with_hint,
pop_any_lane_with_hint). This commit applies the same loop-until-valid
pattern to:

- pop(): the basic three-lane cascading pop
- pop_with_rng(): deterministic RNG tie-breaking across all three lanes
- pop_with_rng_if_due(): deadline-aware three-lane pop
- pop_non_cancel_with_rng(): timed+ready two-lane pop
- pop_non_cancel_with_rng_if_due(): deadline-aware two-lane pop
- pop_cancel_only(): simple cancel lane pop
- pop_timed_only(): deadline-gated timed lane pop

Each method now wraps its lane traversal in a loop, re-checking
scheduled.remove() after each pop and continuing to the next entry if
the task was already cancelled. For multi-lane methods, a stale entry in
one lane correctly falls through to retry the same lane rather than
prematurely advancing to the next lower-priority lane.

Also removes an unnecessary #[inline] on ScheduledSet::remove and
reformats pop_ready_only_with_hint for line length compliance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
